omega_h2.notes: Well above Planck observed value ~0.12; benchmark is deeply off-resonance (Ma=400 >> 2*Mchi=200 GeV), so annihilation is suppressed and universe over-closes.

physics_caveat: 2hdma-004 incident post-mortem: the original SKILL.md narrative claimed 'bbx ~60% dominant' at this parameter point. This was incorrect. Cycle-1 MadDM run (relic.json, run-20260425-current/impl/2hdm-a) shows wphp=49.62%, wmhm=49.62%, bbx=0.65%, ccx=0.06%. The reason bbx is sub-percent: Ma=400 GeV >> 2*Mchi=200 GeV (off-resonance), so a-mediated bb-bar annihilation is suppressed. Instead, chi chi-bar -> W+H- and W-H+ cascade channels dominate (~99.2% combined). This is consistent with off-resonance behavior at this parameter point as understood from 2HDM+a theory (Profumo et al. §III).

source.primary: cycle-1 MadDM run artifact: run-20260425-current/impl/2hdm-a/cycle-1 — S1-snapshot/relic.json (omega_h2=10.5, wphp=49.62%, wmhm=49.62%, bbx=0.65%, ccx=0.06%)
source.paper_note: Post-cutoff paper; not fully verifiable. Cited as context consistent with off-resonance off-shell behavior at Mchi=100, Ma=400 >> 2*Mchi=200 GeV — physics reasoning, not paper quotation.
source.notes: omega_h2 central corrected from 10.15 to 10.493 (cycle-1 relic.json reports 10.5; 10.493 is the deterministic MadDM value from the same run per cycle-1 results.md). dominant_channel corrected from 'bbx' to 'wphp+wmhm'. bbx.central corrected from 0.909 to 0.0065. This rewrite was performed in schema-fix cycle-2 per T-SF-5 reviewer directive.
